scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

3A. The Plaintiff states that the suit value exceeds Rs. 3,00,000/-. However, as the transaction is a personal hand loan between individuals and not a commercial dispute under Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, the mandatory pre-institution mediation under Section 12A is not applicable.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff and the Defendant are known to each other. The Defendant approached the Plaintiff requesting financial assistance by way of a hand loan for his personal requirements.

6. Acting on the request and reposing trust in the Defendant, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000 to the Defendant on 15.03.2024. The said amount was transferred via NEFT bank transfer bearing UTR Number AXIB20240315123456. The proof of the said transaction is annexed herewith as Annexure A.

7. Though no written agreement was executed at the time of advancing the loan, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount and the liability to repay the same through WhatsApp messages exchanged between the parties. The copies of the said digital communications are annexed herewith as Annexure B.

8. The Defendant was obligated to repay the loan amount on or before 20.06.2024. The Defendant failed to repay the amount on the agreed date. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands upon the Defendant for repayment of the dues, but the Defendant postponed the same on various pretexts.

9. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to repay the outstanding dues. The copy of the said legal notice is annexed herewith as Annexure C. Despite the said notice, the Defendant has failed to repay the amount or comply with the requisitions made therein.

DAMAGES

10. By reason of the breach of commitment by the Defendant, the Plaintiff has suffered loss and damage. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000 from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation. The Plaintiff also claims future interest at the same rate from the date of filing of the suit till the date of realisation.

LEGAL BASIS

11. The present cause is governed by Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 73.

12. The present cause is governed by Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, including Section 9, Section 20, Section 34.

CAUSE OF ACTION

13. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

14. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

15. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

16. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

17. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this s
